title: SAP Migration to Cloud
brief: GlobalManufacturing runs SAP ERP on aging on-premises infrastructure. They need to modernize while maintaining performance and reliability. Current environment includes 12 Power7 servers running AIX with SAP ECC. They're evaluating cloud options but concerned about performance, licensing, and migration complexity. Budget approved for 3-year cloud transformation.

questions.title: Identify Customer Priorities
questions.question: Select ALL business outcomes that matter most to this customer:

feedback.correct: Excellent! You identified the key priorities: cost reduction, modernization, compliance, uptime, and disaster recovery.
feedback.partial: Good start, but review the business goals more carefully. Focus on infrastructure modernization and operational improvements.
feedback.incorrect: Review the scenario brief and business goals. This customer needs infrastructure modernization, not AI or developer tools.

questions.title: Recommend IBM Technologies
questions.question: Select ALL IBM technologies you would recommend for this scenario:

options.reasoning: On-prem Power doesn't address cloud modernization goals
Perfect for SAP migration - maintains Power performance in cloud
Foundation platform for Power Virtual Server and other services
Not needed - cloud storage is included with PowerVS
Not applicable for SAP on Power workloads
Not needed for this SAP infrastructure migration
AI is not a current priority for this customer
Not applicable for SAP transactional workloads
Could help with compliance but not essential for initial migration
Security monitoring is valuable but not core to migration
Excellent for optimizing cloud costs and performance
Limited value for traditional SAP monitoring
Useful for financial management but not critical
Critical for successful SAP migration to cloud
Ongoing support for Power Virtual Server environment
Not applicable - customer runs AIX/SAP on Power

feedback.excellent: Perfect! Power Virtual Server is ideal for SAP migration, with Turbonomic for optimization and Consulting for implementation.
feedback.good: Good selections, but consider the complete solution: PowerVS for SAP, Turbonomic for cost optimization, and Consulting for migration expertise.
feedback.needs-improvement: Review the scenario - this is an SAP on Power migration. Power Virtual Server should be your primary recommendation.

questions.title: Architecture Decisions
questions.question: Where should the SAP workload run?

options.reasoning: Doesn't address cloud modernization goals
Best fit - maintains Power performance with cloud benefits
Would require SAP re-platforming and potential performance issues
Loses Power optimization and IBM's SAP expertise

questions.title: Storage Strategy
questions.question: Which storage strategy fits best for SAP on Power Virtual Server?

options.reasoning: Object storage not suitable for SAP database workloads
Optimized for Power workloads with high performance
Defeats purpose of cloud migration
Not integrated with Power Virtual Server

questions.title: Solution Justification
questions.question: Why did you choose IBM Power Virtual Server? Select the BEST reasons:

questions.title: Handle Customer Objection
questions.objection: We've been looking at AWS for our SAP migration. Why should we consider IBM Power Virtual Server instead?
questions.question: Select the BEST response:

options.text: IBM is simply better than AWS for all workloads
options.reasoning: Too generic and not credible

options.text: Power Virtual Server maintains your existing Power/AIX environment while adding cloud benefits. You avoid costly re-platforming, maintain SAP performance, and get flexible consumption. AWS would require x86 migration with performance and compatibility risks.
options.reasoning: Addresses specific technical and business benefits

options.text: IBM Power Virtual Server is cheaper than AWS
options.reasoning: Cost alone isn't the differentiator and may not be true
Factually incorrect - AWS does support SAP

questions.title: Cross-Sell Opportunity
questions.question: Which additional IBM solution would create the MOST value after the SAP migration?

options.reasoning: Limited value for traditional SAP monitoring

options.text: IBM Turbonomic - Continuous cost and performance optimization
options.reasoning: Perfect for optimizing cloud costs and ensuring performance

options.text: IBM Guardium - Database security and compliance
options.reasoning: Valuable but not the highest priority post-migration

options.text: watsonx.ai - AI and machine learning platform
options.reasoning: Not aligned with current priorities

idealSolution.summary: Power Virtual Server provides the ideal path for SAP migration, maintaining Power performance and AIX compatibility while gaining cloud benefits. Turbonomic ensures ongoing optimization, and Consulting ensures successful migration.

title: Financial Services Hybrid Cloud
brief: SecureBank operates critical banking applications across on-premises data centers and AWS. They face increasing security threats, compliance complexity, and rising cloud costs. Core banking runs on IBM Power with Db2, while digital channels run on AWS. They need unified security, better cost management, and want to modernize without disrupting operations.
businessGoals: Enhance security posture across hybrid environment

constraints.budget: High - board-approved security and modernization initiative
constraints.compliance: PCI-DSS, SOC 2, GDPR, regional banking regulations

questions.title: Identify Customer Priorities
questions.question: Select ALL business outcomes that matter most:

questions.title: Recommend IBM Technologies
questions.question: Select ALL IBM technologies you would recommend:

options.reasoning: Already in use for core banking - maintain and optimize
DR and dev/test for Power workloads
Hybrid cloud platform with strong security
High-performance storage for core banking
Modernize digital channels with hybrid deployment
Critical for database security and compliance
Unified security monitoring across hybrid environment
Essential for cost optimization across clouds
Observability for digital channels
Not a current priority
Already in use - optimize and secure
Complex hybrid transformation needs expertise

questions.title: Security Architecture
questions.question: What's the best approach for unified security monitoring?

options.reasoning: Provides unified SIEM across hybrid infrastructure

options.text: AWS Security Hub for AWS, separate tools for on-prem
options.reasoning: Creates security silos
Increases complexity and gaps
Doesn't address security silos problem

questions.title: Application Modernization
questions.question: How should digital channels be modernized?

options.text: Red Hat OpenShift across IBM Cloud and AWS
options.reasoning: Enables hybrid deployment with consistent platform
Misses hybrid cloud benefits and IBM integration
Too disruptive and ignores existing AWS investment
Doesn't modernize applications

questions.title: Solution Justification
questions.question: Why recommend this hybrid cloud approach? Select BEST reasons:

options.text: Unified security with QRadar across all environments
Turbonomic optimizes costs across hybrid infrastructure
Guardium automates compliance for financial regulations

questions.title: Handle Customer Objection
questions.objection: This seems like a lot of IBM products. Won't this add complexity and increase costs?
questions.question: Select the BEST response:

options.text: These solutions are integrated and address your specific challenges: QRadar unifies security, Turbonomic reduces costs across clouds, Guardium automates compliance, and OpenShift modernizes apps. Each solves a critical problem you identified, and they work together to reduce overall complexity.
options.reasoning: Addresses concerns with specific value and integration

options.text: Trust us, IBM knows what's best for financial services
options.reasoning: Dismissive and not credible

options.text: We can phase the implementation over time
options.reasoning: Doesn't address the complexity concern

options.text: You're right, let's just focus on one product
options.reasoning: Doesn't solve their multi-faceted challenges

questions.title: Cross-Sell Opportunity
questions.question: After initial implementation, which solution adds the MOST value?

options.text: watsonx.ai - Add AI capabilities for fraud detection
options.reasoning: Natural next step for financial services with security foundation

options.text: IBM Apptio - Financial management for IT
options.reasoning: Useful but Turbonomic already provides cost optimization
Valuable but QRadar already provides threat detection
Not a priority given current challenges

idealSolution.summary: A comprehensive hybrid cloud solution with unified security (QRadar), cost optimization (Turbonomic), compliance automation (Guardium), and application modernization (OpenShift), while maintaining Power for core banking.
